eventMetadata.context_description: In Liga Nacional de Básquet quarterfinals, Boca Juniors' narrow 70-66 road win in game one at Oberá TC's home court has established a 1-0 series lead, yet trader consensus prices Boca at even 50% implied probability for game two amid Oberá's home-court edge as the fourth seed. The competitive balance stems from Oberá's solid regular-season home form (64% win rate) and urgency to even the best-of-five, offset by Boca's fifth-seed playoff resilience, defensive grit in the opener (forcing turnovers), and slight head-to-head advantage (9-7 all-time). No confirmed injuries; odds could tip toward Boca on momentum from game-one performers or shift to Oberá via adjusted rotations and crowd boost.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus prices Iga Świątek at 30.5% and Aryna Sabalenka at 26.0% to win the 2026 Women's French Open, reflecting a tightly contested favorites market amid mixed clay-court swing results. Świątek, the three-time defending champion prior to Coco Gauff's 2025 triumph, has advanced to the Rome semifinals with straight-set wins over Naomi Osaka and Jessica Pegula, yet her overall 2026 record sits at 14-8 with early inconsistencies tempering expectations despite superior Roland Garros history (72-6 lifetime). World No. 1 Sabalenka boasts a dominant 26-2 season but limited clay breakthroughs, while Elena Rybakina (12.2%) and Gauff (10.5%) gain from steady form and defending status. Madrid's surprise Marta Kostyuk title over Mirra Andreeva highlights the field's volatility, keeping probabilities bunched as draws and weather loom.

markets.question: Will Apple (AAPL) hit (HIGH) $312 in May?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if, at any point during May 2026, any 1-minute candle for Apple (AAPL) has a final "High" price equal to or above the listed price. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Only prices achieved during the regular trading hours of the primary exchange on which the listed security trades (typically 9:30 AM – 4:00 PM ET) will be considered. Prices occurring during pre-market or after-hours trading will not qualify.

Prices will be used exactly as published by Pyth, without rounding.

In the event of a stock split, reverse stock split, or similar corporate action affecting the listed company during the listed time frame, this market will resolve based on split-adjusted prices as displayed on Pyth.

The target price will be adjusted proportionally to reflect any stock splits. Resolution will be based on the historical price data as shown on Pyth after any adjustments have been applied.

The resolution source for this market is Pyth — specifically, the Apple (AAPL) "High" prices available at https://pythdata.app/explore/Equity.US.AAPL%2FUSD, with the chart settings configured for 1-minute candles.

Historical 1-minute candles may be accessed by appending a Unix timestamp (seconds) to the Pyth chart URL using the "t=" parameter. Any timestamp within the listed market time frame may be used to view the relevant candle data (e.g., https://pythdata.app/explore/Equity.US.AAPL%2FUSD?t=1773432000)

If the relevant Pyth data is unavailable due to a system outage, data failure, or other technical disruption that prevents verification of the required 1-minute candle data, the official daily high price published by the primary exchange on which the listed security trades will be used to determine whether the listed price was reached during the applicable trading session.

events.title: What will Apple (AAPL) hit in May 2026?
events.description: What will Apple (AAPL) hit in May 2026?

eventMetadata.context_description: Apple's AAPL shares trade near 52-week highs at approximately $295 per share and a $4.33 trillion market capitalization as of May 13, reflecting trader consensus buoyed by the fiscal Q2 2026 earnings beat reported April 30, with revenue surging 17% year-over-year to $111.2 billion and diluted EPS of $2.01 exceeding analyst estimates amid robust services growth and Apple Intelligence adoption. Analyst price targets average $310, with highs to $400, signaling moderate upside potential driven by AI integrations and potential U.S.-China tariff relief enhancing margins. Key near-term catalysts include the WWDC conference June 8–12, previewing software updates that could sway sentiment before May's end, though macroeconomic pressures like elevated Treasury yields pose risks to valuation multiples.

markets.question: Ceará SC vs. CA Mineiro: O/U 1.5
markets.description:
In the upcoming Copa do Brasil game between Ceará SC and CA Mineiro, scheduled for May 13 at 8:30 PM ET:

This market will resolve to "Over" if Ceará SC and CA Mineiro combine to score 2 or more goals in this game.

If the combined total is less than 2, this market will resolve to "Under".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

If the game is started but not completed, this market will resolve according to the official final score published on cbf.com.br. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: Ceará SC vs. CA Mineiro - More Markets
events.description: More markets for the Copa do Brasil game, scheduled for May 13 at 8:30 PM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: Atlético Mineiro, sitting mid-table in Série A with a 4-2-5 record, hosts Serie B leaders Ceará SC in the Copa do Brasil round-of-16 first leg on April 23 at Arena MRV, where home advantage and a superior head-to-head record—winning nine of 18 prior meetings, including a 1-0 victory at Ceará last year—bolster trader consensus favoring the Galo. Ceará's excellent early-season form remains unbeaten in four Serie B matches, boasting defensive solidity with under 2.5 goals in recent outings, though travel and facing higher-division opposition test their momentum. Atlético faces injury challenges, notably Patrick Silva sidelined by a complex knee ligament issue and Junior Alonso nursing thigh tendinitis, potentially thinning the squad amid a congested schedule including Copa Sudamericana commitments. The two-legged tie's outcome hinges on first-leg result, away goals rule, and lineup confirmations from official reports.

markets.question: Map 2: Odd/Even Total Kills?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Odd" if the total combined kills by both teams in Map 2 is an odd number.

This market will resolve to "Even" if the total combined kills by both teams in Map 2 is an even number.

"Total combined kills" includes all kills recorded during the match across all rounds, including kills during regulation rounds and overtime rounds if applicable. Team kills (friendly fire) and self-inflicted deaths (e.g., falling damage, own grenade) do not count as kills for the purposes of this market.

If no kills are recorded in Map 2, or if Map 2 is canceled (not played at all), or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 2 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 2 is not played because the series result has already been determined (e.g., a team clinches the series before Map 2 is needed), this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 2 is remade, resolution will be based on the remade game only.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from https://hltv.org. However, if https://hltv.org has not published final results within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: Counter-Strike: Imperial Academy vs Infinite (BO3) - United21 Playoffs
events.description:
This market refers to the Counter-Strike Upper bracket quarterfinal 1 match between Imperial Academy and Infinite in the United21 Playoffs, initially scheduled for April 9 at 4:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to "Imperial Academy" if Imperial Academy win the match against Infinite.

This market will resolve to "Infinite" if Infinite win the match against Imperial Academy.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://hltv.org. However, if https://hltv.org has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

In cases where a team’s listed name includes minor discrepancies from the resolution source, this market will resolve based on the underlying real-world match rather than exact name matching. Recognizable abbreviations, alternate or erroneous spellings, sponsor tags, affiliate or academy designations, regional identifiers, and minor formatting differences will be treated as referring to the same team, provided the intended team can be clearly and uniquely identified within the relevant competition. If a listed team name has no reasonable connection to any participating team, or if it matches or could reasonably refer to another team in the same competition such that the intended team cannot be unambiguously determined, this market will resolve 50-50.

eventMetadata.context_description: Imperial Academy faces Infinite in a pivotal best-of-three upper bracket quarterfinal at United21 Season 47 playoffs, with both squads advancing from group stages but showing contrasting momentum. Imperial Academy topped Group A undefeated (2-0 series record, +16 map differential), leveraging a youthful roster featuring 17-year-old rifler sh1Fu and strong performances on Ancient (75% win rate) and Nuke from recent months. Infinite, champions of Season 46 after defeating BIG Academy in the grand final, scraped through Group D (2-1, -9 map diff) hampered by multiple stand-ins including fnl replacing Blytz in playoffs and SHiPZ earlier, exposing lineup instability. Traders weigh Infinite's experience and roster core (mhN1, kreaz, Dytor) against Imperial's hot streak in this online European C-Tier event with $10,000 prize pool, where map vetoes and CS2 meta favoring structured T-sides could prove decisive.

eventMetadata.context_description: Elena Rybakina leads trader consensus for the 2026 Women's Wimbledon at 32.5% implied probability, propelled by her 2022 Grand Slam title on grass, flat-hitting power game with booming serve suited to the low-bouncing surface, and elite 2026 form including Australian Open and Stuttgart triumphs over top-10 foes like Karolina Muchova. World No. 1 Aryna Sabalenka follows at 23.5%, buoyed by her dominant 27-3 record, Miami Sunshine Double completion, and deep Madrid clay run to 25-1 start, though Rybakina's head-to-head wins including the AO final slightly temper her edge. Iga Świątek's 16.4% reflects her 2025 Wimbledon defense and No. 3 ranking, but clay-court focus and patchier hard-court results this year position her behind the big-hitters amid ongoing Rome Masters action.

eventMetadata.context_description: In IEM Atlanta 2026 Group B lower bracket semifinal, a do-or-die BO3 pits #9-ranked Astralis against #26 SINNERS, with the winner advancing to the lower final and top-3 playoff spot on the line while the loser exits. Astralis, fresh off a stunning 0-2 upset loss to Legacy (Dust2 8-13, Nuke 5-13, Inferno 6-13) after dominating Liquid 2-0, faces a resilient SINNERS squad that dropped 1-2 to GamerLegion but bounced back 2-1 over Passion UA earlier today (Dust2 13-8, Mirage 13-9, Overpass 13-8). No head-to-head history exists; Astralis boasts stronger recent pedigree but inconsistent form, while SINNERS excels on Ancient (80% win rate) and Overpass amid stable rosters for both. Trader consensus reflects a razor-thin contest given Astralis' vulnerability and SINNERS' momentum.

markets.question: Will turnout in the Texas Republican Senate primary runoff be below 600,000 voters?
markets.description:
The Texas Senate Republican primary runoff election is scheduled for May 26, 2026.

This market will resolve according to the total vote count in the Texas Republican Senate Primary runoff election (the race’s total canvass votes).

If the reported value falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

If the results of the election aren’t known by November 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to the lowest range bracket.  

The primary resolution source for this market will be information from the State of Texas, such as official statewide results published by the Texas Secretary of State (https://www.sos.state.tx.us/index.shtml); however, an overwhelming consensus of credible reporting may suffice.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on turnout in the Texas U.S. Senate Republican primary runoff prices 1.2–1.5 million votes (35%) and 1.8–2.1 million (32%) as neck-and-neck leaders, driven by the high-stakes May 26 contest between incumbent Sen. John Cornyn and Attorney General Ken Paxton after neither topped 50% in the record GOP primary turnout exceeding 2 million ballots on March 3. Recent University of Houston and other polls show Paxton holding slim leads (48%-45%) among likely voters, spurring intense mobilization and over $110 million in spending—the costliest Senate primary ever—offsetting typical runoff declines to 25-30% of primary levels. No Trump endorsement keeps uncertainty high; early voting starting May 18, final ad blitzes, or consolidation of Wesley Hunt's conservative base behind Paxton could surge volumes toward higher bins, while establishment efforts for Cornyn cap them lower. Intra-party tensions risk downballot drag.

markets.question: Will Microsoft have the top AI model at the end of June 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the company that owns the model with the highest arena rank based on the Chatbot Arena LLM Leaderboard (https://lmarena.ai/) when the table under the "Leaderboard" tab is checked on June 30, 2026, 12:00 PM ET.

Results from the "Rank" column under the "Text Arena | Overall" Leaderboard tab at https://lmarena.ai/leaderboard/text with style control on will be used to resolve this market.

Models will be ordered primarily by their leaderboard rank at the market’s check time. If two or more models are tied on rank, they will be ordered by their Arena score, including any underlying, unrounded, granular values reflected in the data below the leaderboard. If a tie remains, alphabetical order of company names as listed in this market group will be used as a final tiebreaker (e.g., if the two models are tied by exact arena score, “Google” would be ranked ahead of “xAI”). This market will resolve based on the company that occupies first place under this ranking system.

The resolution source for this market is the Chatbot Arena LLM Leaderboard found at https://lmarena.ai/. If this resolution source is unavailable at check time, this market will remain open until the leaderboard comes back online and resolves based on the first check after it becomes available. If it becomes permanently unavailable, this market will resolve based on another resolution source.

markets.question: Will Fikayo Tomori be included in England's official 2026 World Cup squad list?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the listed player is announced as a member of the listed nation’s official squad for the 2026 FIFA World Cup by June 1, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, the corresponding market will resolve to “No”.

Only full squad announcements will be considered. Prior cut lists or previous squad lists other than the officially announced squad lists will not be considered.

If a player is officially announced as part of the squad but is replaced before the nation’s first game for any reason, the corresponding market will still resolve “Yes”.

If the 2026 FIFA World Cup is cancelled, postponed after June 14, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, or the listed player cannot be confirmed as a part of the listed nation’s official squad for the 2026 FIFA World Cup within that timeframe, the corresponding market will resolve to “No”.

The primary resolution source for this market will be information from FIFA (https://www.fifa.com/), however a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

markets.question: Will Elon Musk post 320-339 tweets from May 12 to May 19, 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the number of times Elon Musk (@elonmusk), posts on X from May 12 12:00 PM ET to May 19, 2026 12:00 PM ET.

For the purposes of this market, only main feed posts, quote posts and reposts will count.

Replies will NOT count towards the total - however, replies on the main feed such as https://x.com/elonmusk/status/1786073478711353576 will be counted by the tracker.

Deleted posts will count as long as they remain available long enough to be captured by the tracker (~5 minutes).

Community reposts which are not counted by the tracker not count toward the total.

The resolution source for this market is the 'Post Counter' figure for posts found at https://xtracker.polymarket.com. Individual posts can be viewed by clicking "Export Data". If the tracker does not update correctly in accordance with the rules, X itself may be used as a secondary resolution source.

markets.question: Abstract FDV above $200M one day after launch?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the Fully Diluted Valuation of Abstract's governance token is greater than the value specified in the title 1 day after launch. Otherwise, the market will resolve to "No."

The token must be actively, publicly transferable and tradable to be considered a launch.

The FDV will be determined using the total token supply multiplied by the token price.

"1 day after launch" is defined as 4:00 PM ET on the calendar day following launch. The resolution source for this market is the most liquid price source available. If Abstract doesn't launch a token by December 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "No".

markets.question: Will the US acquire part of Greenland in 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the United States acquires control of any land territory that is part of Greenland by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

Only the transfer of sovereignty, or the acquisition of primary or exclusive jurisdiction or control qualifies.

1. Transfer of Sovereignty: This will qualify if a binding agreement or legal instrument results in a defined area of Greenland coming under the formal sovereignty of the U.S. (e.g., incorporated as a U.S. state, territory, possession, or other U.S. political classification), even if the effective date occurs after the market deadline.

2. Acquisition of Primary or Exclusive Jurisdiction or Control: This will qualify if a binding agreement or legal instrument establishes a defined area in Greenland in which the U.S. has primary or exclusive jurisdiction or control over the territory, such that the ordinary legal authority of Denmark and Greenland do not apply,except by U.S. permission. Such agreements or instruments will qualify even if the effective date occurs after the market deadline. 

3. Use of Force: If the U.S. acquires primary or exclusive jurisdiction or control over a defined area of Greenland through force, this will also qualify. 

An announcement will qualify only if it is accompanied by or consists of a binding agreement or legal instrument  (e.g., enacted legislation, a signed treaty, the signed text of an agreement, or an executive action implementing such an agreement) that unambiguously creates a transfer of sovereignty, or primary or exclusive jurisdiction or control,  even if this transfer or acquisition takes effect after the market deadline. 

Non-binding statements, negotiations, proposals, frameworks, or MOUs will not alone qualify. Basing rights, access agreements, SOFA-type arrangements, COFA-type arrangements, commercial concessions, or other permissions to use land (including leases) will not alone qualify. Any qualifying U.S. jurisdiction or control  in Greenland that existed at market creation will not count as new qualifying control.

Examples of qualifying events include but are not limited to treaty or piece of legislation that makes any portion of Greenland a U.S. territory or possession, even if the handover date for such territory or possession is later); or, a Guantánamo-style arrangement establishing a defined zone in Greenland under exclusive or primary U.S. jurisdiction and control, where Denmark and Greenland’s ordinary legal authority does not apply except by U.S. permission.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the governments of the United States, Denmark, and Greenland; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: President Donald Trump's second-term push for greater U.S. influence in Greenland, citing national security and Arctic resources, has driven diplomatic talks, but trader consensus at 85.5% "No" reflects firm Danish and Greenlandic rejections of any territorial transfer. On May 12, Greenland's leader confirmed U.S. military presence as part of ongoing discussions with American diplomats for new southern bases, building on the existing Thule Air Base, yet emphasized no sovereignty concessions. Earlier 2026 rhetoric, including unverified $700 billion purchase rumors and congressional bills like H.R. 361 authorizing negotiations, faced swift dismissal from Danish Prime Minister Mette Frederiksen and polls showing 85% Greenlandic opposition to U.S. control. Legal obstacles, NATO alliance strains, and Greenland's self-rule status under Denmark underscore significant barriers to acquisition by year-end, tempering expectations despite White House optimism on basing rights.

markets.question: Will the median home value in the DC Metro area be between $566,000 and $572,000 on May 31?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the median home value for all property types in the Washington, D.C. Metro area on May 31, 2026.

If the reported value falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

The resolution source will be official data from the Parcl Labs Sales Price Index for the Washington, D.C. Metro area (Parcl_ID: 2900475). The settlement price will be calculated by multiplying the published price index value (price per square foot) by 1800 square feet, which is the median home size in the Washington, D.C. Metro area. Parcl is set to publish this data on May 31, 2026. If no data for May 31 is released by June 10, 2026, 11:59PM ET, this market will resolve according to the most recently published data. (see: https://app.parcllabs.com/prediction-market-resolutions/36)

markets.question: Will the highest temperature in Karachi be 34°C on May 15?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the Masroor Airbase Station in degrees Celsius on 15 May '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the Masroor Airbase Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/pk/karachi/OPKC.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Pakistan Meteorological Department (PMD) forecasts mainly hot and dry conditions over Karachi through May 18, with daytime highs around 38°C under a persistent high-pressure ridge that inhibits cloud development and convection. After peaking at 44°C earlier this month—the city's hottest since 2018 amid a deadly regional heatwave—temperatures have eased but exceed May's climatological average of 35°C, influenced by continental heating and weak Arabian Sea moisture influx. Ensemble models indicate clear skies and southerly winds moderating May 15's peak to near 36°C, though sea breeze variability adds uncertainty. Resolution hinges on PMD measurements at Jinnah International Airport, with daily bulletins providing key updates for traders.

markets.description:
This market will resolve to the next individual who officially assumes the office of Prime Minister of Romania by December 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET.

To count for resolution, the individual must be formally appointed by the President of Romania and receive a vote of confidence from Parliament, resulting in the official formation of a new government. Any interim or caretaker Prime Minister who does not receive a parliamentary vote of confidence will not count toward the resolution of this market.

If no such Prime Minister takes office by December 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the Government of Romania; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Following Prime Minister Ilie Bolojan's pro-EU cabinet collapse via parliamentary no-confidence vote on May 5 amid disputes over austerity measures and EU fiscal deadlines, President Nicușor Dan has initiated informal consultations and plans official party talks this week, eyeing a new premier nomination next week only with a pre-agreed majority. Trader consensus gives PSD leader Sorin Grindeanu (12.5%) a slim edge over PNL's Cătălin Predoiu (11.5%) in Romania's fragmented parliament, where PSD holds the largest bloc but lacks outright control, while PNL emphasizes reform continuity. Key differentiators include coalition-building prowess—PSD's numerical strength versus PNL's pro-Western appeal—and exclusion of far-right AUR alliances; support may consolidate via center-left/center-right pacts or a technocrat to secure confidence vote and EU funds.

markets.question: Will Abelardo de la Espriella win the 1st round of the 2026 Colombian presidential election?
markets.description:
Colombia's presidential elections are scheduled for May 31, 2026, and a second round (if required) on June 21, 2026, in case no candidate secures more than 50% of the valid votes in the first round.

This market will resolve according to the candidate who receives the greatest number of valid votes in the first round of voting.

If the results of the first round of the Colombian presidential election are not known by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

This market will resolve based on the election results, as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by Colombia's National Civil Registry (Registraduría Nacional del Estado Civil) (https://registraduria.gov.co).

eventMetadata.context_description: Historic Pact candidate Iván Cepeda Castro commands 86.5% trader consensus to win Colombia's presidential first round on May 31, driven by his consistent polling lead around 44% and recent endorsements from dissident Liberals and imploding Green Alliance factions, sealed within the past 48 hours. These coalition boosts capitalize on the party's March legislative gains, despite no outright majority, amid a fragmented opposition where far-right lawyer Abelardo de la Espriella trails at 13.9% on security-focused appeals. Traders price a potential first-round majority for Cepeda despite sub-50% polls, betting on turnout dynamics and opposition infighting, though disputed surveys like AtlasIntel face scrutiny and pre-election threats add uncertainty ahead of the vote.

markets.question: Will West Ham be relegated from the English Premier League after the 2025–26 season?
markets.description:
This is a polymarket to predict which clubs are relegated from the English Premier League after the 2025–26 season.

If the listed club is officially relegated by the English Premier League following the 2025–26 season, this market will resolve to "Yes". Otherwise, it will resolve to "No".

The primary resolution source will be official information from the English Premier League. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Burnley and Wolves face confirmed relegation to the Championship after dismal campaigns, leaving the Premier League's final drop spot as a razor-thin battle between Tottenham Hotspur and West Ham United with two matchweeks remaining. Spurs cling to a two-point lead and better goal difference following their 1-1 draw at Leeds United on May 11, but prolonged home struggles—no league wins at Tottenham Hotspur Stadium since December—have traders wary of an upset. West Ham's recent Brentford defeat deepened concerns, yet their upcoming Newcastle clash offers survival hope before a decisive finale. Nottingham Forest and Leeds have secured safety through recent form surges, intensifying focus on this high-stakes London showdown amid mounting pressure on defenses and goal difference swings.

markets.question: Will the price of Ethereum be above $1,800 on May 15?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the Binance 1 minute candle for ETH/USDT 12:00 in the ET timezone (noon) on the date specified in the title has a final "Close" price higher than the price specified in the title. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

The resolution source for this market is Binance, specifically the ETH/USDT "Close" prices currently available at https://www.binance.com/en/trade/ETH_USDT with "1m" and "Candles" selected on the top bar.

markets.question: Will the highest temperature in Seoul be 20°C on May 15?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the Incheon Intl Airport Station in degrees Celsius on 15 May '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the Incheon Intl Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/kr/incheon/RKSI.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Korea Meteorological Administration's latest short-term forecast, updated May 13, drives trader sentiment toward 26°C or higher at 42% implied probability for Seoul's May 15 high, projecting clear skies, light winds, and ample solar heating under a high-pressure ridge, yielding daytime maxima around 27–28°C. This follows May 13's below-climatological high (near 20°C) due to persistent cloud cover limiting insolation, but model consensus shows rapid warming from southerly air flow and minimal precipitation risk (under 10%). Mid-May norms hover at 22–23°C, yet 2026 spring warmth has pushed analogs higher; uncertainty lingers from potential afternoon clouds or model tweaks in tomorrow's update, clustering odds around 24–25°C as alternatives. KMA advisories expected by evening could shift positions.

markets.question: Will Cerebras not IPO before July 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve based on Cerebras Systems' market capitalization at the closing price on its first day of trading.

As of market creation, the IPO is scheduled to price on May 14 (ET). If no such IPO occurs by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, the market will resolve to "No IPO before July 2026".

Market capitalization expresses the monetary value of a company’s outstanding shares, stated in its pricing currency.

It is calculated as the total number of outstanding shares, multiplied by the official closing share price of the publicly traded class on the first trading day.

If necessary, to accurately capture the company’s total market capitalization, rather than a stock-class-specific market capitalization, the calculation will include all outstanding share classes and apply any stated conversion ratios to the publicly traded class. Where no conversion right exists, such shares will be counted at their stated outstanding amount without discount, unless official filings explicitly specify differently.

The number of outstanding shares will be determined from official company filings or disclosures (e.g., SEC filings). The closing share price on the first trading day will be determined from the primary exchange’s official listing page.

If the relevant value falls exactly between two brackets, this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official company filings and the primary exchange’s official listing page. The market capitalization will be determined through appropriate calculation using the total outstanding shares and the closing price from the first day of trading.

In the event of an interruption in the normal trading session on the specified company’s first day of trading (e.g., a circuit breaker or half-day), the market will resolve according to the official closing price of the abbreviated session. If no such official closing price is published, the market will resolve according to the next trading day on which an official closing price is published, treating that day as the first day of trading for the purposes of this market.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket prices a $60B–$70B closing market cap at 38% implied probability for Cerebras Systems' imminent Nasdaq debut (ticker: CBRS), with $50B–$60B at 26%, reflecting a modest first-day premium atop the company's freshly upsized IPO range of $150–$160 per share for 30 million shares—implying a fully diluted $48.8 billion valuation and $4.8 billion raise amid 20x oversubscription reported over the past 48 hours. Surging AI chip demand, fueled by 76% revenue growth to $510 million in 2025, GAAP profitability, a $10 billion-plus OpenAI compute supply deal, and Amazon Web Services as its first hyperscaler customer, underpins the positioning, though lofty multiples versus Nvidia invite volatility. Pricing concludes May 13, with trading commencing May 14.

The announcement of a qualifying consumer hardware product within the market timeframe will suffice, regardless of whether or when the product is released.

markets.question: Will the Bank of Russia make no change to the key rate after the June Meeting?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the change in the key rate resulting from the Bank of Russia’s June meeting, relative to the level it was prior to this meeting.

The resolution source for this market is information released by the Bank of Russia after its June 19, 2026 meeting as listed on the official Bank of Russia calendar: https://www.cbr.ru/eng/dkp/cal_mp/#t13

This market may resolve as soon as the Bank of Russia’s press release for their June 19, 2026 meeting with relevant data is issued. If no decision on the key rate is issued by the end date of the next scheduled meeting, this market will resolve to the "No change" bracket.

eventMetadata.context_description: Polymarket traders assign an 84% implied probability to a Bank of Russia key rate decrease at the June 19 meeting, extending the easing cycle after the central bank's eighth consecutive 50 basis point cut to 14.5% on April 24 amid moderating domestic demand and inflation steadying at 5.7% as of late April. Declining household inflation expectations to 12.9% in April further supports this consensus, aligning with the bank's baseline forecast of a 14.0–14.5% average rate for 2026. Yesterday's Ministry of Economic Development revision—slashing GDP growth to 0.4% while lifting inflation to 5.2%—reinforces growth-supportive policy amid contraction signals, though upside price risks cap aggressive easing with 16.5% odds for no change.

markets.question: Will Sweden win on 2026-06-25?
markets.description:
In the upcoming game, scheduled for June 25, 2026
If Sweden wins, this market will resolve to "Yes".
Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".
If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.
If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve "No".

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus slightly favors Japan at 48% implied probability for their FIFA World Cup Group F clash against Sweden on June 25 at AT&T Stadium, driven by their 18th FIFA ranking versus Sweden's 38th and impressive late-March friendlies—1-0 wins over England at Wembley and Scotland—highlighting high-pressing transitions via Kaoru Mitoma and Takefusa Kubo. Sweden sits at 39.5% after playoff qualification triumphs, including Viktor Gyokeres' hat-trick in a 3-1 win over Ukraine and a late 1-0 victory against Poland, though a poor group stage and April 14 friendly loss to Denmark temper enthusiasm. The 36.5% draw pricing reflects injury uncertainties—Japan's Wataru Endo (foot) and Sweden's Dejan Kulusevski (knee)—in a neutral-venue matchup with sparse head-to-head history.

markets.question: Will the Democratic Party win the CA-24 House seat?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the party of the candidate who wins the CA-24 congressional district seat in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. The midterm elections will take place on November 4, 2026.

​A candidate's party will be determined by their ballot-listed or otherwise identifiable affiliation with that party at the time all of the 2026 House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources. A candidate without a ballot-listed affiliation to either the Democrat or Republican parties will be considered a member of one of these parties based on the party with which they most recently expressed their intent to caucus at the time all of the House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources.

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the United States government, specifically the Federal Election Commission (https://www.fec.gov/).

eventMetadata.context_description: **Incumbent Democrat Salud Carbajal commands trader consensus at 93.5% implied probability to win California's 24th Congressional District House seat, driven by the district's strong Democratic partisan lean (D+18), his consistent large-margin victories including 2024 reelection, and superior incumbency advantages like fundraising and name recognition ahead of the June 2 top-two primary.** Recent candidate profiles highlight challengers including Republican Bob Smith and Democrats Sarah Bacon and Helena Pasquarella, but none pose a credible threat to Carbajal advancing; the general election on November 3 likely pits him against Smith in this safe Democratic battleground spanning Santa Barbara to San Luis Obispo counties. Structural factors favor Democrats amid favorable voter registration. Scenarios to challenge this include a primary upset excluding Carbajal, a major scandal or health issue, or a massive Republican midterm wave shifting turnout in swing areas—though historical base rates for incumbent reelection in such districts exceed 95%, rendering Republican odds slim at 5.5%.

events.title: Wisconsin Governor Democratic Primary Winner
events.description:
This market will resolve according to the winner of the Democratic Primary for Governor of Wisconsin, scheduled to take place on August 11, 2026. Resolution will be based on the overall winner of the primary, including any potential second round or run-off.

If no 2026 Wisconsin Gubernatorial Democratic Primary takes place, this market will resolve to “Other.”

The resolution source for this market will be the first official announcement of the results from the Wisconsin Democratic Party; however, an overwhelming consensus of credible reporting may suffice.

eventMetadata.context_description: Former Lt. Gov. Mandela Barnes leads trader consensus in the Wisconsin Democratic gubernatorial primary at an implied 54.5% probability, propelled by his unmatched name recognition from serving as lieutenant governor and his narrow 2022 U.S. Senate loss in this battleground state, signaling strong electability against likely Republican nominee Tom Tiffany. State Rep. Francesca Hong follows at 34.8%, sustaining momentum from March polls like Patriot Polling where she briefly topped the field amid progressive enthusiasm and grassroots fundraising claims. Current Lt. Gov. Sara Rodriguez holds 5.5% leveraging her office, while the crowded 13-candidate field sees others trail amid high undecided rates in February's Marquette Law School survey. Post-April Supreme Court election focus and recent forums on data centers and clean energy keep the August 11 primary fluid, with Barnes' recent Nation endorsement on fair maps bolstering his position.

markets.question: Will Michael Echols be the Republican nominee for LA-05?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the candidate who wins the nomination for the Republican Party to contest the LA-05 congressional district seat in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. The Republican primary will take place on May 16, 2026.

If no nominee is announced by November 3, 2026, 11:59PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of official Republican sources, including https://www.rnc.org/.

Any replacement of the nominee before election day will not change the resolution of the market.

eventMetadata.context_description: State Senator Blake Miguez leads trader consensus at 63.5% implied probability in the LA-05 Republican primary due to his early February endorsement from President Trump, dominant fundraising with $4 million cash on hand reported in mid-April, and top position in the Rainey Center Freedom Project poll from early April showing him at 23% among likely voters ahead of State Representative Michael Echols at 20%. Echols holds second at 20.5% bolstered by his role as House Republican Caucus Chairman and seven-figure war chest, while others trail amid fragmented support in the seven-candidate field. Late April candidate forums at sites like Live Oak High and ULM reinforced the two-man dynamic without major polling shifts, as the May 16 primary nears.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the Esenboğa Intl Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/tr/%C3%A7ubuk/LTAC.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket overwhelmingly favors 22°C (99.9% implied probability) as Ankara's highest temperature on May 13 at Esenboğa International Airport—the official resolution station—driven by tight convergence in the latest ECMWF and GFS model runs alongside Turkish State Meteorological Service (MGM) guidance projecting daytime highs of 21–22°C under partly cloudy conditions with light westerly winds capping solar heating. Intraday observations from Wunderground confirm temperatures peaking near 22°C (around 71°F), aligning with mid-May climatological norms where average highs hover at 22°C amid stable high-pressure influence. Realistic challenges include sudden clearing for enhanced insolation or thunderstorm downdrafts pushing to 23°C, though low sun angle late in the day limits upside; final MGM/Wunderground data expected post-midnight.

markets.question: Will Africa win the 2026 FIFA World Cup?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the continent of the country that wins the 2026 FIFA World Cup, currently scheduled for June 11-July 19, 2026.

For example, if France wins the tournament, the market will resolve to Europe.

If the 2026 FIFA World Cup is cancelled, postponed after December 31, 2026, or there is otherwise no winner declared within that timeframe, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The definitive source of the continent for each country will be World Population Review (https://worldpopulationreview.com/country-rankings/list-of-countries-by-continent).

The primary resolution sources for this market will be official information from FIFA (https://www.fifa.com/) and the World Population Review; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

events.description:

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus prices Europe at 72.5% implied probability to win the 2026 FIFA World Cup, driven by its unmatched depth across 16 qualified teams including top-ranked Spain, France, England, Germany, Portugal, and Netherlands—bolstered by recent UEFA playoff triumphs for Bosnia and Herzegovina, Czechia, Sweden, and Türkiye in early April that locked in a powerhouse contingent despite Italy's absence. South America's 20.5% reflects defending champions Argentina, Brazil, Colombia, Ecuador, Paraguay, and Uruguay's consistent excellence in CONMEBOL's grueling round-robin, though limited to six spots. Africa's 10 qualifiers like Morocco and Senegal offer breakout potential amid expanded slots, but historical knockout struggles cap them at 3.5%; Asia (Japan, South Korea), North America (hosts USA, Canada, Mexico), and Oceania (New Zealand) trail due to talent gaps versus European and South American elites, with group draw dynamics further emphasizing continental hierarchies.

markets.question: Will Renan Santos finish in second place in the first round of the 2026 Brazilian presidential election?
markets.description:
A presidential election is scheduled to take place in Brazil on October 4, 2026.

This market will resolve according to the listed candidate who receives the second-most valid votes in the first round of this election.

The named candidates will be primarily ranked by the number of valid votes received in the specified election. If two or more candidates are tied on valid votes, ties will be broken by alphabetical order of the candidates' last names. This market will resolve to the candidate that occupies the second-highest finishing position after applying this ranking.

If the result of this election isn't known definitively by June 30, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, the market will resolve to "Other".

This market will resolve based on the result of the election, as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the Brazilian government, specifically the Superior Electoral Court (Tribunal Superior Eleitoral, TSE) (e.g., https://dadosabertos.tse.jus.br/).

eventMetadata.context_description: Recent polls consistently position incumbent President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva as the first-round leader for Brazil's October 4, 2026, presidential election, with Senator Flávio Bolsonaro entrenched in second place at 34-40%, driving his 67% implied probability among traders. Surveys like Futura (May 4-8: Lula 38.1%, Flávio 37.4%) and AtlasIntel (April 22-27: Lula 46.6%, Flávio 39.7%) show narrow leads but Flávio far ahead of rivals such as Ronaldo Caiado (5-6%) or Renan Santos (up to 5.3%). Lula's 16.5% for second reflects his incumbency edge, while Flávio leverages the enduring Bolsonaro brand amid his father's ineligibility. Ciro Gomes' May 11 decision to run for governor instead narrows the field further, with campaign momentum key ahead of the vote.

markets.question: Will Liberal Alliance be part of the next Government of Denmark?
markets.description:
A Parliamentary election is scheduled to be held in Denmark on March 24, 2026.

This market will resolve to “Yes” if the listed political party is included in the first Danish government formed after the 2026 Danish parliamentary election. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

This market may resolve once the first government is officially confirmed following the appointment of the Prime Minister and ministers by the monarch after the 2026 Danish parliamentary election, with ministers sworn in under non-caretaker circumstances.

A party will only be considered part of the government if it participates in the governing coalition and provides at least one cabinet minister. Parties that merely support the government from outside the cabinet (e.g., through parliamentary support agreements or similar arrangements) without holding a cabinet post will not qualify.

If no government is formed, or the results are not known definitively by January 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “No”.

This market will resolve based on a consensus of credible reporting. In case of ambiguity, this market will resolve based on official information from the Government of Denmark.

eventMetadata.context_description: Denmark's snap general election on March 24, 2026, resulted in a hung Folketing, with the red bloc securing a plurality of 86 seats but falling short of the 90 needed for a majority, while the blue bloc holds 78 and centrists like the Moderates are pivotal. Caretaker Prime Minister Mette Frederiksen's Social Democrats, with 38 seats, led initial coalition talks but failed to secure agreement, prompting King Frederik X on May 8 to appoint Venstre's Troels Lund Poulsen as formateur for potential center-right or blue-bloc arrangements, including Venstre, Conservatives, Liberal Alliance, Danish People's Party, and Moderates. Negotiations resumed May 11 amid economic and Greenland policy disputes, with minority government precedents common; traders eye Moderates' stance as decisive for the next coalition's composition.

markets.question: Map 1: Odd/Even Total Kills?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Odd" if the total combined kills by both teams in Map 1 is an odd number.

This market will resolve to "Even" if the total combined kills by both teams in Map 1 is an even number.

"Total combined kills" includes all kills recorded during the match across all rounds, including kills during regulation rounds and overtime rounds if applicable. Team kills (friendly fire) and self-inflicted deaths (e.g., falling damage, own grenade) do not count as kills for the purposes of this market.

If no kills are recorded in Map 1, or if Map 1 is canceled (not played at all), or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 1 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 1 is not played because the series result has already been determined (e.g., a team clinches the series before Map 1 is needed), this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 1 is remade, resolution will be based on the remade game only.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from https://hltv.org. However, if https://hltv.org has not published final results within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: Counter-Strike: Spirit Academy vs Wave Esports (BO3) - Exort Series Main Stage
events.description:
This market refers to the Counter-Strike Round 1 match between Spirit Academy and Wave Esports in the Exort Series Main Stage, initially scheduled for April 16 at 11:30AM ET.

This market will resolve to "Spirit Academy" if Spirit Academy win the match against Wave Esports.

This market will resolve to "Wave Esports" if Wave Esports win the match against Spirit Academy.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://hltv.org. However, if https://hltv.org has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

In cases where a team’s listed name includes minor discrepancies from the resolution source, this market will resolve based on the underlying real-world match rather than exact name matching. Recognizable abbreviations, alternate or erroneous spellings, sponsor tags, affiliate or academy designations, regional identifiers, and minor formatting differences will be treated as referring to the same team, provided the intended team can be clearly and uniquely identified within the relevant competition. If a listed team name has no reasonable connection to any participating team, or if it matches or could reasonably refer to another team in the same competition such that the intended team cannot be unambiguously determined, this market will resolve 50-50.

eventMetadata.context_description: Spirit Academy holds a clear edge over Wave Esports in this BO3 matchup at Exort Series Main Stage, driven by their recent 2-1 head-to-head victory on April 16 in Exort #25 round 1, securing maps on Dust2 (16-13), Mirage (16-16? wait, per details 13-6/9-13? adjusted), and Ancient amid a flawless 3-0 Swiss run (+23 round differential) that advanced them to playoffs. Wave finished 0-3 (-38 RD) with stand-ins like cMS, exposing vulnerabilities in defense. Spirit's roster—VILBy, mazay, Kiryasoo, kidofpain, s1nside—excels in structured aggression suiting current CS2 meta, while Wave's Katalic, sheezy, miksozzz, Belof lineup lacks depth. Traders eye map vetoes, pistol rounds, and potential roster tweaks, with no major patches or changes in the past 30 days.

markets.question: Al Shabab Saudi Club vs. Al Ittihad Saudi Club: Both Teams to Score
markets.description:
In the upcoming Saudi Professional League game between Al Shabab Saudi Club and Al Ittihad Saudi Club, scheduled for April 10 at 2:00 PM ET:

This market will resolve to "Yes" if both Al Shabab Saudi Club and Al Ittihad Saudi Club each score at least one goal during the game.

This market will resolve to "No" if either team fails to score (i.e., if one or both teams finish with zero goals).

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

If the game is started but not completed, this market will resolve according to the official final score published on slstat.com. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: Al Shabab Saudi Club vs. Al Ittihad Saudi Club - More Markets

eventMetadata.context_description: Al Ittihad sit sixth in the Saudi Pro League table with 42 points from 26 matches, but their recent form has faltered with just one win in the last five games (1W-2D-2L), exacerbated by the winter departures of stars Karim Benzema to Al Hilal and N'Golo Kanté, leaving gaps in attack and midfield amid fan frustration ahead of a King's Cup semi-final. Al Shabab, 12th on 29 points, host at SHG Arena on April 10 showing sharper scoring edge lately (three wins in five, 12 goals), buoyed by home strength despite mid-table position and forward Mohammed Harbush sidelined long-term with a cruciate injury. Al Ittihad hold a strong head-to-head edge, unbeaten in the last five meetings, setting up a competitive Riyadh clash influenced by rest advantages and tactical adjustments.

eventMetadata.context_description: Hong Kong Observatory observations confirm the city's air temperature reached 31°C at the Observatory by 4 p.m. HKT on May 13, locking in the market-implied certainty of a daily high of 31°C or higher amid sunny periods driven by an anticyclone aloft. Only an unprecedented drop in late-afternoon readings across all stations could challenge this, though model consensus and current trends make it implausible; final daily maximums will be summarized by HKO tomorrow.

markets.question: Will Donald Trump announce Todd Blanche as the next United States Attorney General by June 30?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the first individual Donald Trump announces as his pick to be United States Attorney General. 

An announcement from Donald Trump or the Trump administration stating their intent to nominate a specific individual for United States Attorney General will suffice to resolve this market, regardless of whether a formal nomination actually occurs.

Qualifying announcements must explicitly present the relevant individual as the nominee or future nominee for Attorney General. Announcements of acting or interim appointments, or announcements which merely reveal potential candidates, will not qualify. Media reports, speculation, or other unofficial information will not qualify.

A formal presidential nomination of an individual to be United States Attorney General will also suffice to resolve this market. Formal nominations are defined as the submission of a nomination message to the U.S. Senate.

If Donald Trump does not announce his pick for the next United States Attorney General by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "No announcement by June 30".

The resolution source for this market will be official information from Donald Trump and the Trump Administration; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

If Donald Trump does not announce his pick for the next United States Attorney General by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "No announcement by June 30".

The resolution source for this market will be official information from Donald Trump and the Trump Administration; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus favors no announcement of a permanent Attorney General nominee by June 30 at 50%, reflecting over five weeks since President Trump's April 2 ouster of Pam Bondi and elevation of Todd Blanche to acting AG, with no formal nomination submitted to the Senate amid reports of satisfaction with Blanche's performance. Lee Zeldin tops named contenders at 17.5% following early post-firing discussions between Trump and the EPA administrator, though allies urge him to remain there; Blanche trails at 11.5% as the seamless interim choice potentially serving long-term without confirmation. Harmeet Dhillon (7.3%), current Assistant AG for Civil Rights, and Texas AG Ken Paxton (5.5%) draw support from Trump ally networks, but Senate confirmation hurdles, including swing votes like Sen. Thom Tillis, temper expectations for a swift pick.

markets.question: Will SpaceX's market cap be between $2.0T and $2.5T at market close on IPO day?
markets.description:
This market will resolve based on SpaceX's market capitalization at the closing price on its first day of trading.

If no SpaceX IPO occurs by December 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, the market will resolve to "No IPO before 2028".

Market capitalization expresses the monetary value of a company’s outstanding shares, stated in its pricing currency. It is calculated as the number of shares outstanding multiplied by the closing share price on the first trading day.

If the relevant value falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

Resolution will be based on the primary exchange’s official listing page. In the event that the relevant figure is not displayed, another reliable source will be used.

In the event of an interruption in the course of the normal trading session on SpaceX’s first day of trading (e.g., a circuit breaker or half-day), the market will resolve according to the official closing price of the abbreviated session. If no such official closing price is published, the market will resolve according to the next trading day on which an official closing price is published, treating that as the first day of trading for purposes of this market.

Starship's rapid testing cadence and reusability breakthroughs bolster competitive dominance over rivals like Blue Origin, while orbital satellite deployments expand broadband reach, but skeptics cite $800 billion private valuations and macroeconomic headwinds. Key swing factors include the imminent S-1 prospectus by May 22 revealing financials, June roadshow pricing, and FAA approvals for Starship, with resolution hinging on sustained launch cadence and AI datacenter ambitions.

markets.question: Will the lowest temperature in Tokyo be 16°C on May 15?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the lowest temperature recorded at the Tokyo Haneda Airport Station in degrees Celsius on 15 May '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the lowest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the Tokyo Haneda Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/jp/tokyo/RJTT.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Japan Meteorological Agency's May 13 forecast indicates a 15°C low for Tokyo on May 15 under partly cloudy skies with light winds and 30% precipitation chance, driving trader consensus toward 15°C (28%) and 16°C (27%) as leading outcomes. This tight clustering reflects ensemble model spreads—JMA, GFS equivalents, and others—varying by 1°C due to uncertainty in overnight cloud cover, which modulates radiative cooling in the shallow boundary layer, and Tokyo's urban heat island effect tempering extremes. Recent observations show May 13 highs near 27°C dropping to 17°C overnight into May 14, signaling mild spring conditions without incoming cold fronts. JMA's May 14 update and new model runs could tip the balance as resolution approaches.

markets.question: Will Donald Trump post 180-199 Truth Social posts from May 8 to May 15, 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the number of times Donald Trump (@realDonaldTrump), posts on Truth Social between May 8, 12:00 PM ET and May 15, 2026, 12:00 PM ET.

For the purposes of this market, only main feed posts, quote posts and reposts will count.

Replies will NOT count towards the total - however, replies which are recorded on the main feed will be counted by the tracker.

Deleted posts will count as long as they remain available long enough to be captured by the tracker (~5 minutes).

The resolution source for this market is the "Post Counter" figure for posts found at https://xtracker.polymarket.com. Individual posts can be viewed by clicking "Export Data". If the tracker does not update correctly in accordance with the rules, Truth Social itself may be used as a secondary resolution source.

markets.question: Will Tesla, Inc. (TSLA) hit (LOW) $360 in May?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if, at any point during May 2026, any 1-minute candle for Tesla, Inc. (TSLA) has a final "Low" price equal to or below the listed price. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Only prices achieved during the regular trading hours of the primary exchange on which the listed security trades (typically 9:30 AM – 4:00 PM ET) will be considered. Prices occurring during pre-market or after-hours trading will not qualify.

Prices will be used exactly as published by Pyth, without rounding.

In the event of a stock split, reverse stock split, or similar corporate action affecting the listed company during the listed time frame, this market will resolve based on split-adjusted prices as displayed on Pyth.

The target price will be adjusted proportionally to reflect any stock splits. Resolution will be based on the historical price data as shown on Pyth after any adjustments have been applied.

The resolution source for this market is Pyth — specifically, the Tesla, Inc. (TSLA) "Low" prices available at https://pythdata.app/explore/Equity.US.TSLA%2FUSD, with the chart settings configured for 1-minute candles.

Historical 1-minute candles may be accessed by appending a Unix timestamp (seconds) to the Pyth chart URL using the "t=" parameter. Any timestamp within the listed market time frame may be used to view the relevant candle data (e.g., https://pythdata.app/explore/Equity.US.TSLA%2FUSD?t=1773432000)

If the relevant Pyth data is unavailable due to a system outage, data failure, or other technical disruption that prevents verification of the required 1-minute candle data, the official daily low price published by the primary exchange on which the listed security trades will be used to determine whether the listed price was reached during the applicable trading session.

markets.question: Will there be no Somaliland parliamentary election before 2027?
markets.description:
Parliamentary elections are to be scheduled to be held in Somaliland in late March 2026.

This market will resolve according to the political party that wins the greatest number of seats in the next Somaliland House of Representatives (Golaha Wakiilada) election. 

If no election takes place by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "No election before 2027".

If an election takes place but the results are not known definitively by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will remain open until the certified results are released. If the results are not known definitively by March 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

In the event of a tie between multiple parties for the most seats won, this market will resolve in favor of the party that received a greater number of valid votes. In the event that results in a tie, this market will resolve in favor of the party or coalition whose listed abbreviation appears first in alphabetical order.

This market's resolution will be based solely on the number of seats won by the named party in the Somaliland Parliament. 

This market will resolve based on the results of this election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the Somaliland government, specifically the Somaliland National Electoral Commission (Komishanka Doorashooyinka Qaranka) (slnec.net).

If an election takes place but the results are not known definitively by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will remain open until the certified results are released. If the results are not known definitively by March 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

In the event of a tie between multiple parties for the most seats won, this market will resolve in favor of the party that received a greater number of valid votes. In the event that results in a tie, this market will resolve in favor of the party or coalition whose listed abbreviation appears first in alphabetical order.

This market's resolution will be based solely on the number of seats won by the named party in the Somaliland Parliament. 

This market will resolve based on the results of this election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the Somaliland government, specifically the Somaliland National Electoral Commission (Komishanka Doorashooyinka Qaranka) (slnec.net).

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket heavily favors no Somaliland parliamentary election before 2027 at 72%, driven by the House of Elders' April 28, 2026, decision to extend the House of Representatives' term by 27 months amid logistical challenges cited by the National Electoral Commission, postponing polls originally slated for May 2026. This marks another delay in a history of postponed votes, including the 2021 parliamentary contest after a 16-year gap, eroding momentum despite President Abdirahman Mohamed Abdullahi (Cirro)'s pledges for timeliness. Among parties, Waddani leads at 23.4% implied probability, buoyed by Cirro's recent presidential win over Kulmiye, while Justice and Welfare (UCID) trails at 5.2% and former ruling party Kulmiye at 1.6%. Recent Hargeisa mayoral election tensions underscore clan dynamics potentially complicating voter registration and security for national polls.

markets.question: Will Google (GOOGL) close above $380 on May 13?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the official closing price for Alphabet Inc. (GOOGL) on May 13 is higher than the listed price. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No."

If the final session is shortened (for example, due to a market-holiday schedule), the official closing price published for that shortened session will still be used for resolution.

If no official closing price is published for that session (for example, due to a trading halt into the close, system issue, delisting, or other disruption), the market will use the last valid on-exchange trade price of the regular session as the effective closing price.

In the event of a stock split, reverse stock split, or similar corporate action affecting the listed company during the listed time frame, this market will resolve based on split-adjusted prices as displayed on Yahoo Finance. AccuWeather's latest run projects a 24°C high with RealFeel nearing 27°C under mostly sunny conditions and light ENE winds at 12 mph, while timeanddate.com and Weather Channel ensembles cluster at 22-23°C, moderated by Busan's coastal sea breeze that historically caps mid-May peaks around 22-23°C. Recent observations—May 13 mid-day temps hitting 24°C despite a 22°C forecast—have boosted optimism for 25-26°C if full diurnal heating occurs without haze or marine layer intrusion; key differentiator is afternoon cloud timing, with KMA hourly updates expected this evening potentially resolving the 1°C spread.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket has locked in 19°C as the lowest temperature at Shanghai Pudong International Airport on May 13, 2026, with 100% implied probability, driven by official early-morning observations confirming a minimum of 19°C around 05:00 local time amid lingering humidity from recent frontal passages. Subsequent hourly data from the Shanghai Meteorological Observatory and international models (e.g., ECMWF, GFS) show daytime readings climbing to 30°C under partly cloudy skies and southerly winds, with evening forecasts holding above 20°C due to urban heat island effects and overcast conditions inhibiting radiative cooling. Historical May climatology supports this, as Shanghai's average low is 16–18°C, but recent warming after early-month cold snaps has stabilized minima. A realistic challenge would require sudden clearing and northerly gusts tonight, though current dew points near 19°C make sub-19°C unlikely before midnight resolution. Official daily summary expected post-00:00 UTC May 14.

markets.question: Will UBS or any of its underwriting affiliates serve as the lead underwriter in SpaceX’s initial public offering?

markets.question: Will Opendoor Technologies Inc. (OPEN) hit (LOW) $3.50 Week of May 11 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if, at any point during the week of May 11 2026, any 1-minute candle for Opendoor Technologies Inc. (OPEN) has a final "Low" price equal to or below the listed price. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Only prices achieved during the regular trading hours of the primary exchange on which the listed security trades (typically 9:30 AM – 4:00 PM ET) will be considered. Prices occurring during pre-market or after-hours trading will not qualify.

Prices will be used exactly as published by Pyth, without rounding.

In the event of a stock split, reverse stock split, or similar corporate action affecting the listed company during the listed time frame, this market will resolve based on split-adjusted prices as displayed on Pyth.

The target price will be adjusted proportionally to reflect any stock splits. Resolution will be based on the historical price data as shown on Pyth after any adjustments have been applied.

The resolution source for this market is Pyth — specifically, the Opendoor Technologies Inc. (OPEN) "Low" prices available at https://pythdata.app/explore/Equity.US.OPEN%2FUSD, with the chart settings configured for 1-minute candles.

Historical 1-minute candles may be accessed by appending a Unix timestamp (seconds) to the Pyth chart URL using the "t=" parameter. Any timestamp within the listed market time frame may be used to view the relevant candle data (e.g., https://pythdata.app/explore/Equity.US.OPEN%2FUSD?t=1773432000)

If the relevant Pyth data is unavailable due to a system outage, data failure, or other technical disruption that prevents verification of the required 1-minute candle data, the official daily low price published by the primary exchange on which the listed security trades will be used to determine whether the listed price was reached during the applicable trading session.

markets.question: Ukraine agrees not to join NATO before 2027?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Ukraine publicly agrees not to join NATO by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

An official pledge by Ukraine not to join NATO will qualify for a “Yes” resolution whether as a unilateral announcement or part of an agreement with the Russian Federation.

Any agreement or pledge made before the resolution date of this market will qualify, regardless of if/when the agreement goes into effect.

An agreement by Ukraine not to join NATO for any amount of time will count (e.g. If Ukraine not to join NATO for 10 years this will qualify).

An agreement by Ukraine not to join NATO as a precondition of a more comprehensive peace process or deal will qualify, even if the agreement is not finalized or part of a formalized peace deal. The September 8, 1995 “Agreed Basic Principles” between Bosnia and Yugoslavia which recognized the borders and sovereignty of Bosnia and Herzegovina, and was later formalized through the Dayton Peace Agreement is an example of a qualifying agreement. 

The primary resolution source for this market will be an official announcement by the Ukraine, however an overwhelming consensus of credible reporting confirming a qualifying agreement has been reached will also count.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus prices "No" at 82.5%, reflecting Ukraine's refusal to issue an official pledge forgoing NATO membership before 2027 amid stalled Russia-Ukraine peace talks. Despite President Zelenskyy's December 2025 offer to drop NATO ambitions for Western security guarantees, no agreement materialized, with February 2026 Geneva negotiations yielding low expectations over territorial and neutrality demands. Recent developments, including NATO's April $60 billion 2026 aid pledge and Zelenskyy's May requests for details on Putin's May 9 ceasefire proposal, underscore continued alliance support without concessions. Finnish President Stubb's calls for direct European-Russia diplomacy highlight negotiation pressures, but Ukraine prioritizes air defense and arms, maintaining its NATO path absent a comprehensive deal.
